title: CORE Worker Declaration
description: Constitutional declaration of a Worker or ShopManager entity. Existence in .intent/workers/ is the sole source of constitutional standing.

id.description: Unique identifier for this worker declaration. e.g.  workers.proposal_consumer_worker

authority.description: Workers operate at policy or code authority. Never meta or constitution.

rationale.description: Human-readable explanation of status decisions, e.g. why a worker is paused.

uuid.description: Permanent constitutional UUID. All proposals and blackboard entries must carry this UUID.

class.description: Worker class determines its relationship to the Blackboard. supervision is reserved for ShopManagers.

responsibility.description: Single sentence declaring the one thing this worker is responsible for. No conjunctions.

phase.description: The CORE phase in which this worker is permitted to act. Subset of `phase`; see worker_phase in enums.json.

permitted_tools.description: Canonical tool keys this worker is permitted to invoke.

tools.description: Alias for permitted_tools. Accepted for compatibility.

rule_namespace.description: Per ADR-091 D1 (widened semantics). Audit topic this worker emits findings under — formerly 'rule ID prefix'. Where the topic matches a declared rule namespace (the AuditViolationSensor case), rule resolution proceeds: all rules whose ID starts with this prefix are in scope, resolved dynamically from IntentRepository at runtime. Where the topic does not match any rule prefix (e.g. test.coverage, coherence.incoherence), resolution returns an empty set and the sensor's run() does not iterate over rules — but the topic still anchors the canonical subject format declared in ADR-091 D2.

artifact_type.description: Per ADR-091 D1. Array of registered artifact_type ids from .intent/artifact_types/ that this sensor observes. Single-element arrays are the common case; multi-element arrays declare joint observation (e.g. [python, test] for sensors that cross-reference instances of multiple types). Cross-validation invariant (ADR-091 D4): every element must appear in that artifact_type's supported_sensors array. The conditional `if class == sensing then required` lands in the top-level allOf block (active after #570 closed 2026-06-05).

approval_required.description: Whether proposals from this worker require human approval before execution.

schedule.description: Self-scheduling contract. When present, the worker manages its own asyncio loop. The Orchestrator enforces this contract via last_heartbeat monitoring.

max_interval.description: Cycle cap — maximum seconds between consecutive cycle starts (and equivalently between heartbeats, since each cycle begins with a heartbeat). Per ADR-103: the runtime enforces this via `sleep(max(max_interval - elapsed, 0))`, so next cycle starts at `max(elapsed, max_interval)`. The operator declares the cadence; the runtime adapts sleep to honor it. When observed cycle exceeds this cap (× 1.1 jitter tolerance), `runtime.worker_max_interval_within_observed` fires — meaning real work has grown past the cap, not that the rule is too tight.

glide_off.description: Jitter tolerance in seconds added to max_interval before the liveness check raises worker.silent. Defaults to 10% of max_interval if absent. Per ADR-103 D2: this is measurement-jitter accommodation (scheduler delays, DB write latency), not a separate tolerance budget operators may spend. The rule's × 1.1 multiplier serves the same purpose at the 24h-aggregation cadence.

batch_size.description: Number of items to process per run cycle. Workers that process collections in batches read this to control throughput.

files_per_cycle_max.description: Blast-bound cap on files processed per cycle. Workers managing destructive autonomous loops declare this constitutionally; absent declaration refuses worker registration (ADR-069 D3 / ADR-070 D8 no-runtime-fallback). Files beyond the cap are deferred to the next cycle; a coherence finding is posted when the cap is hit so the rate limit is observable on the audit surface.

class.description: Python class name within the module.

requires_core_context.description: Whether this worker requires a CoreContext instance injected at construction.

requires_dedicated_process.description: When true, this worker must run in its own asyncio event loop (its own OS process). The lightweight daemon refuses to host it; a dedicated systemd unit runs it alone. Authority: ADR-081. Default (absent): false. Workers whose per-cycle execution includes long synchronous CPU-bound stretches (full-codebase AST walks, graph computation) must declare true — their presence in a shared loop starves peer workers' cooperative scheduling. Network-await work (e.g. embedding via httpx.AsyncClient) is NOT in this category and must declare false even when its observed cycle gaps are large — its inflation is starvation by AST-walker peers, not loop monopolization. See ADR-081 D2 for the classification criterion.

params.description: Arbitrary constructor kwargs passed to the worker at instantiation time by the daemon loader. Use for worker-specific configuration that cannot be expressed through standard schema fields (e.g. dry_run: false to mark sensor findings as actionable by downstream remediators). Values are merged into the kwargs dict after standard fields (declaration_name, rule_namespace, core_context) are resolved, so they may not override those.

config.description: Worker-specific runtime configuration read by the worker via self._declaration.get('config', {}). Values are not passed to the constructor — they are read directly from the declaration at runtime.

allOf.description: ADR-091 D1 conditional enforcement (active after #570 closed 2026-06-05): sensing-class workers MUST declare mandate.scope.artifact_type and mandate.scope.rule_namespace. The conditional was staged at Phase 1 to keep the daemon loadable while embedders/crawlers/transformers carrying class:sensing were reclassified out. With #570 closed, every class:sensing worker is a true artifact observer and the requirement lands.
